questions.scenario: A worker overwrites `receipts/order-417.pdf` with the wrong file. Which protection is most directly useful for recovering the earlier object content?

options.text: Blob versioning, if it was enabled before the overwrite.
A SQL index on the orders table.

questions.explanation: Versioning protects previous blob versions from overwrite mistakes. SQL, compute scale, and partition keys do not recover blob bytes.

questions.scenario: The storage account holds receipts and temporary import files. Which pairings make sense?

options.text: Use soft delete to give accidental blob deletes a recovery window.
Use lifecycle rules to delete temporary import files after their product lifetime.
Use lifecycle rules to delete customer receipts before the business retention period.
Pair versioning with lifecycle rules so old versions do not grow forever.

questions.explanation: Protection and cleanup should match the data promise. Lifecycle rules are dangerous when they delete data before the business is done with it.
